info.title: Xeize Security Open-API
info.description:
Open API for comprehensive security analysis and management platform.

Xeize provides enterprise-grade security scanning, vulnerability management,
and compliance reporting across your entire development lifecycle.

## Authentication
This API uses API key authentication. You can authenticate by:
- Using the `Authorization: Bearer {your-api-key}` header

## Support
Contact support@xeize.dev

## Endpoints
All Open-API(V1) endpoints are prefixed with `/open-api/v1/` and are designed for
third-party integrations and automated workflows.

tags.name: LLM Token Management
tags.description: APIs for retrieving LLM authentication tokens for vulnerability analysis

tags.name: False Positive Management
tags.description: APIs for managing false positive vulnerabilities, fix suggestions, and project prioritization

tags.name: Health & Monitoring
tags.description: Health check and API monitoring endpoints for external integrations

tags.name: CVE Information
tags.description: APIs for retrieving Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) information including details, CVSS/EPSS scores, and vulnerability status

tags.name: Project Management
tags.description: APIs for registering and managing Git repository projects

tags.name: Git Repository Access
tags.description: APIs for accessing git repository information and credentials

tags.name: Integration Management
tags.description: APIs for managing Git platform integrations (GitHub, GitLab, etc.)

put.description:
Send heartbeat to maintain the lock timeout for locked issues (false positive or fix suggestion processing).
This endpoint extends the lock duration to prevent it from expiring while processing is ongoing.

This endpoint works for both:
- False positive review locks
- Fix suggestion processing locks

Use this endpoint to:
- Maintain lock on issues during long-running analysis
- Prevent lock expiration during manual review
- Ensure exclusive access to locked issues

parameters.name: lockKey
parameters.description: Lock key to send heartbeat for

200.description: Successfully sent heartbeat and extended lock timeout

post.description:
Register a new Git repository project for security analysis and vulnerability scanning.

Repository Identifier Format:
- GitHub: 'owner/repository' (e.g., 'group/project')
- GitLab: 'group/project' or 'group/subgroup/project'
- Must match exact repository path on the Git platform

Validation Requirements:
- Repository must exist and be accessible via integration credentials
- Branch specification is optional (defaults to repository's default branch)
- Integration credentials are validated before project creation

post.summary: Lock issues for fix suggestion processing
post.description:
Locks and retrieves issues requiring fix suggestions for a specific project.
This endpoint creates a lock on available issues that are marked as true positives or inconclusive and returns them to prevent concurrent processing by other systems.

Important:
- Issues are locked for a limited time and require heartbeat to maintain the lock
- Use PUT /locks/{lockKey}/heartbeat to extend the lock duration
- The lock_key in the response must be used for subsequent operations
- Locked issues will not appear in other lock requests until the lock expires or is released
- If no issues are available, an empty list will be returned with a valid lock

201.description: Successfully created lock and retrieved issues for fix suggestions (may be empty if no issues available)

post.description:
Locks and retrieves pending false positive issues for a specific project for processing.
This endpoint creates a lock on available issues that are marked as potential false positives and returns them to prevent concurrent processing by other systems.

Important:
- Issues are locked for a limited time and require heartbeat to maintain the lock
- Use PUT /locks/{lockKey}/heartbeat to extend the lock duration
- The lock_key in the response must be used for subsequent operations
- Locked issues will not appear in other lock requests until the lock expires or is released
- If no issues are available, an empty list will be returned with a valid lock

201.description: Successfully created lock and retrieved pending issues (may be empty if no issues available)

patch.description:
Update fix suggestions and status for specific vulnerability issues.
This endpoint allows you to provide automated fix suggestions for vulnerabilities that have been triaged as true positives or inconclusive during false positive analysis.

Important: This operation requires the lock key obtained from the lock request and will only update issues that are currently locked with the provided lock key. This ensures exclusive access and prevents concurrent modifications.

The operation will automatically release the lock on all successfully processed issues, making them available for other processes to access.

Fix Suggestion Task Status options:
- PENDING: Fix suggestion generation is still in progress
- DELAYED: Fix suggestion generation delayed due to constraints (e.g., LLM rate limits)
- COMPLETED: Fix suggestion has been successfully generated
- FAILED: Fix suggestion generation failed
- SKIPPED: Fix suggestion generation was skipped

patch.summary: Update false positive issue status and triage
patch.description:
Update the false positive task status, triage result, and review details for specific vulnerability issues.
This endpoint allows you to mark the processing status (pending, completed, etc.) and classify issues as false positives, true positives, or inconclusive after review and analysis.

Important: This operation requires the lock key obtained from the lock request and will only update issues that are currently locked with the provided lock key. This ensures exclusive access and prevents concurrent modifications.

The operation will automatically release the lock on all successfully processed issues, making them available for other processes to access.

Task Status options:
- PENDING: Issue is still under review
- DELAYED: Issue review delayed due to constraints (e.g., LLM rate limits, resource limitations)
- COMPLETED: Issue review has been completed
- FAILED: Review process failed
- SKIPPED: Issue was skipped during review

Triage options:
- FALSE_POSITIVE: Issue is confirmed as a false positive
- TRUE_POSITIVE: Issue is a valid vulnerability, not a false positive
- INCONCLUSIVE: Issue could not be determined

200.description: Successfully updated issue false positive status and triage

get.description:
Retrieve vulnerabilities with paging and optional filtering.
This endpoint returns a paginated list of vulnerabilities that can be filtered by project, status, severity, and priority score.

Use this endpoint to:
- Get all vulnerabilities across projects or for a specific project
- Filter vulnerabilities by status (e.g., OPEN, RESOLVED)
- Filter vulnerabilities by severity (e.g., CRITICAL, HIGH)
- Filter vulnerabilities by priority score range (minScore, maxScore)
- Filter vulnerabilities by detection date range (detectedFrom, detectedTo)

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: Filter by project ID. If not provided, returns vulnerabilities across all projects.

parameters.name: detectedFrom
parameters.description: Filter by detection start date (createdAt >=), ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: detectedTo
parameters.description: Filter by detection end date (createdAt <=), ISO 8601 format

get.description:
Retrieve an LLM authentication token for the specified purpose.
This endpoint returns the configured LLM token that should be used for the requested purpose.

Available purposes:
- VULNERABILITY_ANALYSIS: Token for analyzing and processing vulnerability data
- CODE_REVIEW: Token for code review and analysis tasks

If no token is configured for the specified purpose, a 404 Not Found response will be returned.

The returned token includes all necessary information to authenticate with the LLM provider, including the provider type, endpoint URL, and authentication token value.

parameters.description: Purpose for which to retrieve the LLM token

200.description: Successfully retrieved LLM token for the specified purpose

get.description:
Retrieve all currently registered Git platform integrations.
This endpoint returns a list of all active integrations configured in the system, including GitHub and GitLab integrations.

The response includes integration details such as:
- Integration ID for referencing in other operations
- Integration name and Git platform type (GITHUB/GITLAB)
- Server URL
Use cases:
- Retrieve integration IDs for project registration

Security Note:
- Only active integrations are returned in the response

This is typically the first endpoint you should call when integrating with our API.

get.description:
Retrieve git repository URL and Personal Access Token (PAT) for repository access.
This endpoint provides the necessary credentials to access the git repository for code analysis, false positive verification, and security scanning.

Security Note:
- PAT tokens are sensitive and should be handled securely
- Tokens may have limited permissions and expiration dates
- Use HTTPS for all git operations with the provided credentials
- Store credentials securely and never log them in plain text

Use cases:
- Clone repository for local analysis
- Access specific commits for vulnerability verification
- Perform git blame analysis for false positive investigation

parameters.description: Project ID to get git credentials for

get.description:
Retrieve projects with true positive or inconclusive vulnerabilities requiring fix suggestions.
This endpoint returns the top N projects that contain vulnerabilities marked as true positives or inconclusive and require automated fix suggestions. Projects are automatically prioritized based on internal algorithms considering factors like issue count, severity, and project importance.

Use this endpoint to:
- Get a prioritized list of projects requiring fix suggestions
- Understand workload distribution across projects
- Plan fix suggestion generation activities

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of projects to return (1-100)

200.description: Successfully retrieved projects pending fix suggestions

get.description:
Retrieve projects with pending false positive issues, ordered by internal priority.
This endpoint returns the top N projects that contain vulnerabilities marked as potential false positives but are still in pending status awaiting review. Projects are automatically prioritized based on internal algorithms considering factors like issue count, severity, and project importance.

Use this endpoint to:
- Get a prioritized list of projects requiring false positive review
- Understand workload distribution across projects
- Plan false positive review activities

parameters.description: Maximum number of projects to return (1-100)

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information about a specific Common Vulnerability and Exposure (CVE) by its identifier.
This endpoint returns comprehensive CVE details including:
- CVE status and publication information
- Multilingual vulnerability descriptions
- CVSS v3/v4 scoring with severity ratings
- EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System) metrics

CVE IDs follow the format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN or CVE-YYYY-NNNNNNN
Examples: CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2022-22965

OpenApiProjectRegistrationRequest.description: Request to register a new Git repository project for security analysis

branch.description: Specific Git branch to analyze. If not provided, the repository's default branch will be used. Use this to analyze specific development branches or release branches.

integrationId.description: Integration ID associated with this project. Null if the project was uploaded manually

repository.description: Git repository identifier. Null if the project was uploaded manually.

branch.description: Git branch being analyzed (defaults to repository's default branch if not specified)

projectId.description: Project ID to get issues from

count.description: Maximum number of issues to return and lock (1-100)

scannerType.description: Type of security scanner that identified the issue

result.description: Review result or reason for the current status

learnings.description: List of learnings associated with this issue
example:
- The project uses parameterized queries in most places but missed this specific endpoint.
- User input validation is implemented at the API gateway level.
- Database access follows strict patterns except for dynamic reporting queries.

FixSuggestionIssuesResponse.description: Response containing locked issues for fix suggestions

issues.description: List of locked issues requiring fix suggestions

totalCount.description: Total number of issues available for fix suggestions

requestedCount.description: Number of issues requested in the lock

lockExpiresAt.description: Lock expiration timestamp in ISO 8601 format

scannerType.description: Type of security scanner that identified the issue

totalCount.description: Total number of pending issues available

requestedCount.description: Number of issues requested in the lock

lockExpiresAt.description: Lock expiration timestamp in ISO 8601 format

FixSuggestionIssueUpdate.description: Fix suggestion update for an issue

fixSuggestion.description: Generated fix suggestion for the vulnerability
fixSuggestion.example: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation. Replace 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ' + userId with prepared statements using placeholders.

fixCode.description: Generated fix code for the vulnerability

UpdateFixSuggestionRequest.description: Request to update fix suggestions for issues

lockKey.description: Lock key from the original lock request

issues.description: List of issues to update with fix suggestions

failedCount.description: Number of issues that failed to update

failedIssues.description: List of issue IDs that failed to update

updatedAt.description: Update operation timestamp in ISO 8601 format

meta.description: Additional metadata for the issue update

learnings.description: List of learnings for the issue update
learnings.example:
["- The project uses a `TemporaryFile` abstraction to consistently handle files and their MIME types, centrally enforcing safe output in API views.
- No custom or user-tainted MIME type setting logic was found for exported Excel files; all are set to the Microsoft Excel xlsx MIME string.
- This structure robustly prevents HTTP header injection in the reflected section of code.
- Business logic for salary contract export templates enforces strong typing and prohibits dynamic MIME or filename generation from tainted/client sources.
- Sanitizer or validation logic for fileType is unnecessary due to use of constants."]

UpdateIssueRequest.description: Request to update false positive issue status

lockKey.description: Lock key from the original lock request

remediatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vulnerability was remediated in ISO 8601 format

CvssDetails.description: CVSS scoring details for a specific version

vector.description: CVSS vector string representing attack complexity and impact

severity.description: Qualitative severity rating based on CVSS score

CvssInfo.description: CVSS (Common Vulnerability Scoring System) scoring information

score.description: EPSS score - probability of exploitation in the next 30 days (0.0-1.0)

percentile.description: EPSS percentile - percentage of vulnerabilities with lower EPSS scores (0.0-1.0)

en.example: Apache Log4j2 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 (excluding security releases 2.12.2, 2.12.3, and 2.3.1) JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers when message lookup substitution is enabled. From log4j 2.15.0, this behavior has been disabled by default. From version 2.16.0 (along with 2.12.2, 2.12.3, and 2.3.1), this functionality has been completely removed. Note that this vulnerability is specific to log4j-core and does not affect log4net, log4cxx, or other Apache Logging Services projects.

ko.example: Apache Log4j2 2.0-beta9부터 2.15.0까지 버전(보안 릴리스인 2.12.2, 2.12.3, 2.3.1 제외)에서는 설정(configuration), 로그 메시지, 파라미터에 사용되는 JNDI 기능이 공격자가 조작하는 LDAP 및 기타 JNDI 관련 엔드포인트에 대해 충분한 보호를 제공하지 않습니다. 공격자가 로그 메시지나 메시지 파라미터를 조작할 수 있는 경우, 메시지 조회 치환(message lookup substitution)이 활성화되어 있을 때 LDAP 서버에서 로드된 임의의 코드를 실행할 수 있습니다. Log4j 2.15.0 버전부터는 기본 설정으로 이 동작이 비활성화되었으며, 2.16.0 버전(및 2.12.2, 2.12.3, 2.3.1 보안 릴리스)부터는 이 기능이 완전히 제거되었습니다. 이 취약점은 log4j-core에만 해당하며, log4net, log4cxx 또는 기타 Apache Logging Services 프로젝트에는 영향을 주지 않습니다.

bearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication using Authorization header. Example: `Authorization: Bearer your-api-key-here`
